Q4 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • Expert • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The question has 4 statements S1 to S4 followed by 4 conclusions in the form of options ‘a’ to ‘d’.
Identify the conclusion which logically follows from at least one of the 4 statements.
S1 : All senior schools are colleges.
S2 : No university is a play school.
S3 : Some primary schools are senior schools.
S4 : All colleges are universities.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Given that all senior schools are colleges and some primary schools are senior schools which leads to the conclusion that “all those primary schools which are senior schools are colleges” i.e. some of the colleges are primary schools.

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In a Markov chain, the future state depends only on the current state, independent of the previous state.
S2 : Monte Carlo methods involve random sampling when direct sampling is not feasible.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• A Markov chain follows the Markov property, meaning the probability of transitioning to the next state depends only on the current state and not on the sequence of states that preceded it. So, S1 is correct.
• Monte Carlo methods use random sampling from a distribution to approximate results,
especially when direct or analytical sampling from the distribution is difficult or impossible. So, S2 is correct.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In a game tree, if the current node’s value is greater than or equal to β, then the MIN player will prune the search.
S2 : If a node’s value is less than or equal to α, the MAX player will prune the search.
Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The MIN player prunes the search when the current node's value is greater than or equal to β because it indicates that the MAX player has a better choice available. So, S1 is correct.
• The MAX player prunes the search if a node’s value is less than or equal to a because it means that the MIN player has a better option. So, S2 is correct.

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
If we insert keys 45, 48, 55 in same order, then on insertion of key 55, root will be overflow and new level will be created.

Q20 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A binary tree T has n leaf nodes. The number of nodes of degree 2 in T is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Because for 2 degree node, every time 2 leafs are added then number of nodes with degree 2 increases by 1.
Thus, number of nodes with degree 2 is always one less than number of leafs present in tree.

Q57 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) An undirected graph is said to be Hamiltonian if it has a cycle containing all the vertices. Any DFS tree o Hamiltonian graph must have depth V – 1.
(b) The depth of any DFS tree rooted at a vertex is atleast as depth of any BFS tree rooted at the same vertex.
(c) DFS traversal and BFS traversal on a complete binary tree of height atleast 2 will always give the same output.
(d) For a directed graph, the absence of back edges in a DFS tree can have cycle.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• An undirected graph is said to be Hamiltonian if it has a cycle containing all the vertices. Any DFS tree o Hamiltonian graph must have depth V – 1. So, option (a) is correct.
• Depth of any vertex in BFS always less than equals to depth of same vertex in DFS. So,
option (b) is correct.
• DFS traversal and BFS traversal on a complete binary tree of height atleast 2 will give different output since BFS visited all the adjacent vertices first but DFS visited node in depth wise.
So, option (c) is incorrect.
• For a directed graph, the absence of back edge in DFS tree means no cycle present. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q60 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) The largest element in the min heap tree will always occupy a leaf node.
(b) Given n elements, a min heap can be created in Ο(n2) time.
(c) Randomized quick sort is not an improvement over quick sort (traditional) for an already sorted array.
(d) Inorder traversal of a binary search tree is always ascending order.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Since, the largest element should remain of the tree, any element less than it will trigger a heapify operation so the largest element will definitely come to leaf node level. So, option (a) is correct.
• Min heap + c = Ο(n). So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Randomize quick sort handles sorted array better than traditional quick sort. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Inorder traversal of a binary search tree is always ascending order. So, option (d) is correct.

Q61 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) The non-clustered index is created to improve the performance of frequency used queries covered by clustered index.
(b) B-tree is used instead of B+, when sequential access to key value is required.
(c) Cross product and natural join, both are commutative and associative.
(d) Consider the expression, G Natural Join. If a and b have disjoint heading then this expression is equivalent to a product b. If a and b have same heading then this expression is equivalent to a INTERSECTION b.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
• This is false because the non-clustered index is created to improve the performance of frequently used queries not covered by clustered index. A non-clustered index is stored at one place and table data is stored in another place. This is similar to a textbook where the book content is located in one place and the index is located in another. While a clustered index defines the order in which data is physically stored in a table. Table data can be sorted in only one way, therefore, there can be only one clustered index per table. Therefore non-clustered index
improve the performance of the queries not covered by the clustered index. So, option (a) is
incorrect.
• B-tree is never used for sequential access of records. So, option (b) is incorrect.
